Defendant Crane Co.¿S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Fox of K&L Gates (415-882-8017) for Crane Company Defendant and Moving Party / Bruce Jackson of Keller Fishback & Jackson LLP (818-342-7442) for Dennis Hruska Plaintiff and Opposing Party

Counsel for defendant states he did not become aware of Court's tentative ruling until 8/31/11 and would like to argue. Court finds the procedure to be made aware of tentative ruilngs and thereby notify the court of the desire to argue was not followed and neither will it allow argument.

COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS:
Defendant Crane Co.'S Motion For Summary Adjudication Defendant's motion is granted as to issue 1 (punitive damages) and denied as to issues 2 and 3 (false representation and intentional tort). As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue of material fact whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant's objection to plaintiffs' supplemental interrogatory response is sustained and its objection to its responses to General Order interrogatories is overruled. As to false representation and intentional tort, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting those causes of action. Defendant failed to demonstrate that it propounded special discovery asking for all facts, witnesses and documents supporting plaintiffs' false representation and intentional tort causes of action, or that any responses thereto were factually devoid. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Judge Teri L. Jackson, CSR: T. Willat-Harrison, #4609 (503)

Off Calendar Apr-22-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on defendant Crosby Valve, Inc.'s Motion to Compel Further Responses from Plaintiffs to Written Discovery, and for Sanctions Against Plaintiffs and their Attorneys, Keller, Fishback, Jackson, LLP.
Dept. 611, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Joe Vickstein, #4780.

Cassiana Aaronson, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Francis Hruska, individually and as successor-in-interest to Dennis Hruska, Donald Hruska and Douglas Hruska.
Fiona J. Miller, Esq. appeared for defendant and moving party, Crosby Valve, Inc.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, defendant's motion is GRANTED. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose defendant Crosby Valve, Inc.'s motion. Plaintiffs' only objection is that the motion was not filed in a timely fashion. Plaintiffs shall provide defendant with discovery responses within ten days from today.

Defendant's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Within ten days from date of entry of order, plaintiffs shall pay defendant Crosby Valve, Inc. sanctions in the amount of $1,760.00.

Counsel for defendant Crosby Valve, Inc. shall submit a proposed Order After Hearing to the Court no later than 5:00 p.m. on Monday, April 25, 2011.
